fields.description:
# Make the API-stability version references version-agnostic (remove drift-prone markers)

Adopt the mainstream OSS pattern (FastAPI/attrs/httpx): state the stability **policy**
without naming a current version in prose, and point readers to the CHANGELOG / GitHub
Releases for live numbers. This removes the duplicated `currently X.Y.Z` package-version
marker in `docs/api-stability.md` — and with it the drift it caused and the guard that
policed it (`tests/unit/test_version_sync.py`) — so no version must be hand-bumped in the
doc each release. (Design Option A from the api-stability version-marker analysis; Option E
— VCS-derived versioning — is parked separately as idea deficient-eastmost-hoopoe.)

## Why

`docs/api-stability.md` hard-codes `(currently 0.8.0)`, duplicating `pyproject.toml`'s
version. That duplication silently drifted before (the doc once read 0.6.0 while the package
was 0.7.1), which is why `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` exists to fail CI on a mismatch.
The specific number adds nothing over the "0.x ⇒ any minor may break" statement that carries
the real meaning; the live version belongs in the CHANGELOG / Releases, which the release
flow already maintains. Removing the marker eliminates the duplication at its source, so the
consistency test is correctly removed WITH it (removing the test while keeping the marker
would reopen the drift bug). The same anti-pattern already bit the same file a second time:
the Event-schema row reads `SCHEMA_VERSION (currently **3**)` while the code is at **5** — a
live, wrong literal — so this change de-literals those parentheticals too. Popular
actively-maintained projects write pre-1.0 stability policy this way: FastAPI's "About
FastAPI versions" and attrs' "Backward Compatibility" name no current version and defer to
the changelog.

## What / Scope

- `docs/api-stability.md`:
  - Reword the opening so it states the 0.x SemVer policy WITHOUT a `currently X.Y.Z` version
    literal, and links to the CHANGELOG and GitHub Releases for the current version.
  - De-literal the Event-schema row: replace `SCHEMA_VERSION (currently **3**, in
    rebar/reducer/_version.py)` and `EXPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION (currently 1)` with references to
    those constants in their source files WITHOUT a hard-coded number (fixes the stale `3`,
    which is actually 5, and removes the same drift-prone anti-pattern).
  - Keep the rest of the page (the per-surface stability matrix content) unchanged.
- `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` — DELETE. Its sole purpose was to keep the now-removed
  package-version marker in sync; with no marker there is nothing to check.
- `docs/releasing.md` — remove ONLY the two dangling references added when cutting v0.8.0:
  drop `docs/api-stability.md` from the step-1 `git add` bump command, and delete the
  `test_version_sync.py` / `(currently X.Y.Z)` mentions in that step. **Preserve** the
  unrelated 1.0-declaration section's reference to `docs/api-stability.md` (the "remove the
  Pre-1.0 caveat" instruction near line 434) — it is not about the version marker.
- Out of scope: any change to `pyproject.toml`/`server.json` version handling (that is
  Option E, parked as idea deficient-eastmost-hoopoe).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/api-stability.md` contains NO `currently <number>` literal of any kind — neither
  the package version nor the schema versions (verify:
  `! grep -Eiq 'currently[* _]*[0-9]' docs/api-stability.md`) — and DOES link to the
  changelog/releases for the version (verify: `grep -Eiq 'CHANGELOG|releases' docs/api-stability.md`).
- [ ] The Event-schema row still names `SCHEMA_VERSION` and its source file but with no
  hard-coded number (verify: `grep -q 'SCHEMA_VERSION' docs/api-stability.md && grep -q '_version.py' docs/api-stability.md`).
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` no longer exists (verify:
  `test ! -e tests/unit/test_version_sync.py`).
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md`: no `test_version_sync` reference remains, its step-1 `git add`
  command no longer stages `docs/api-stability.md`, and no `currently X.Y.Z` literal remains;
  BUT the 1.0-declaration reference to api-stability.md is preserved (verify:
  `! grep -q 'test_version_sync' docs/releasing.md && ! grep -Eq 'git add[^\n]*api-stability' docs/releasing.md && ! grep -Eq 'currently [0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' docs/releasing.md && grep -q 'api-stability' docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass (the deleted test is gone; a repo-wide
  `grep -rl 'test_version_sync' --include='*.py'` over `src`/`tests` returns nothing), and the
  change lands on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`).
